audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:had/hadden + past participle]] and [[grammar:toen + past clause]]. Use [[grammar:had]] or [[grammar:hadden]] plus a participle for something that was already complete in the past. Use [[grammar:toen]] for when in a specific past situation.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:had/hadden + past participle]]: The pluperfect locates a completed event anterior to a past reference point.; [[grammar:toen + past clause]]: [[grammar:Toen]] anchors the clause to a definite past reference time and contrasts with recurring [[grammar:als]].

audienceSections.normal:
#### had/hadden + past participle

Use [[grammar:had]] or [[grammar:hadden]] plus a participle for something that was already complete in the past. Example: [[grammar:De vrijwilligers hadden de werkplaats al schoongemaakt.]]

#### toen + past clause

Use [[grammar:toen]] for when in a specific past situation. Example: [[grammar:Toen het trammuseum opende, stond de collectie klaar.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### had/hadden + past participle

The pluperfect locates a completed event anterior to a past reference point. Literal example: [[grammar:De vrijwilligers hadden de werkplaats al schoongemaakt.]]

#### toen + past clause

[[grammar:Toen]] anchors the clause to a definite past reference time and contrasts with recurring [[grammar:als]]. Literal example: [[grammar:Toen het trammuseum opende, stond de collectie klaar.]]

audienceSections.normal: The pluperfect places one completed event before another past event. [[grammar:Toen]] refers to a specific past time, unlike recurring [[grammar:als]].
audienceSections.expert: The pluperfect places one completed event before another past event. [[grammar:Toen]] refers to a specific past time, unlike recurring [[grammar:als]].

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Opa Henk werkte vroeger als tramchauffeur.]] — Grandpa Henk used to work as a tram driver.
- [[grammar:Jaren later hielp hij bij de restauratie van een oude tram.]] — Years later he helped with the restoration of an old tram.
- [[grammar:De vrijwilligers hadden de werkplaats al schoongemaakt.]] — The volunteers had already cleaned the workshop.
- [[grammar:Pieter had nieuwe lampen voor de collectie besteld.]] — Pieter had ordered new lights for the collection.
- [[grammar:Toen de tram aankwam, was het trammuseum nog gesloten.]] — When the tram arrived, the tram museum was still closed.
- [[grammar:Het vrijwilligersteam had de opening zorgvuldig voorbereid.]] — The volunteer team had carefully prepared the opening.
- [[grammar:Lotte had informatie bij elke tram gelegd.]] — Lotte had placed information by every tram.
- [[grammar:De collectie bevatte tweeduizendvijfhonderd foto’s en documenten.]] — The collection contained two thousand five hundred photos and documents.
- [[grammar:Toen het trammuseum opende, stond de collectie klaar.]] — When the tram museum opened, the collection was ready.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk had zijn oude uniform meegenomen.]] — Grandpa Henk had brought his old uniform.
- [[grammar:Veel bezoekers herkenden hem van vroegere ritten.]] — Many visitors recognized him from earlier journeys.
- [[grammar:Pieter had koffie en brood voor de vrijwilligers geregeld.]] — Pieter had arranged coffee and bread for the volunteers.
- [[grammar:Toen de eerste dag eindigde, waren alle rondleidingen vol.]] — When the first day ended, all the guided tours were full.
- [[grammar:De restauratie had veel tijd gekost, maar het resultaat was prachtig.]] — The restoration had taken a great deal of time, but the result was beautiful.
- [[grammar:Lotte schreef de bezoekersaantallen op.]] — Lotte wrote down the visitor numbers.
- [[grammar:Opa Henk wist dat de lange voorbereiding de moeite waard was geweest.]] — Grandpa Henk knew that the long preparation had been worthwhile.
